reasoning: The question essentially asks if the stock price of WAB on 2025-04-20, 2025-05-13, 2025-07-12, and 2025-10-10 will be higher than the price on 2025-04-13. Since 2025-04-13 is a Sunday, the market close price to compare against is Friday, 2025-04-11, which was $170.90. This transforms the question into a standard 'will the stock be higher than its current closing price after N days' problem, which implies looking at the random walk process of stock prices over various horizons. The statistical model provided indicates a prior probability around 0.4 due to short-term downwards momentum over the last three months, though WAB recently bottomed at $159.42 and has rallied slightly back to $170.90. The stock will release earnings on April 23, 2025, right after the first resolution date. This means the first resolution date sits precisely between today and earnings, so the price should largely be a standard drift, perhaps with a slight upward or downward tilt depending on pre-earnings sentiment. Given the recent steep drop from $185, the immediate term has poor momentum. So for 2025-04-20, I set a probability slightly under 0.5 (0.48) to reflect the bearish short-term momentum. For the May, July, and October dates, time allows typical stock drift and equity risk premium to begin dominating the short-term noise. Thus, uncertainty increases (probabilities move toward 0.5 and eventually eclipse it) as horizons get longer: 0.49 for May, 0.51 for July, 0.52 for October.
